note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of; verify_freshness.py cross-checks these against an independent Yahoo re-fetch and the decision_card value. FILED data (10-K/10-Q line items) is NOT here -- it rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. This dossier is a v1.1 re-validation of the v1 run: the price was independently RE-FETCHED and confirmed CORRECT (3 sources agree at 577.22, last close 2026-06-18 <= as_of), so the v1 price stands (contrast NVDA, whose v1 price was the 52wk low). The only stale LIVE-qualitative item corrected was the FTC antitrust framing (see active_litigation).

live_fields.value: FTC v. Meta (IG/WhatsApp monopolization): on 2026-06 status is a trial-level WIN for Meta now under FTC appeal -- NOT an imminent breakup. 2025-11-18 Judge Boasberg (DDC) ruled Meta does NOT hold a monopoly (faces TikTok/YouTube competition) and will NOT force a spin-off of Instagram/WhatsApp. 2026-01 FTC filed notice of appeal to the DC Circuit; 2026-06-01 24+ state AGs backed the appeal. => antitrust forced-breakup is a LONG-TAIL appellate tail risk that already cleared the trial in Meta's favor, not an open near-term threat. Corrects the 2026-06-19 inversion_map F4 framing 'no recent ruling' (there WAS a recent ruling, and Meta won it). Direction de-risks F4; verdict unaffected (F4 was always a non-veto long-tail, and breakup is now less likely, reinforcing WATCH-not-REJECT).

sources.url: States Back FTC's DC Circ. Appeal In Meta Monopoly Case

live_fields.value: Q2 2026 revenue guidance $58-61B (assumes ~2% FX tailwind). FY2026 capex guidance RAISED to $125-145B incl finance leases (from prior $115-135B), citing higher component pricing + data-center build-out; FY2026 total expense guidance $162-169B (unchanged); expects 2026 operating income above 2025. Confirmed still current as of 2026-06 (set at Q1 2026 print 2026-04-29; no superseding guide since).
